items.name: DEPTH_SCOPED_KEYS set verification
items.description: Verify all 13 depth-scoped keys in DEPTH_SCOPED_KEYS set correctly produce @dN suffixed versions at depth > 0. Keys: current_depth, iteration_count, final_response_text, last_repl_result, should_stop, repl_submitted_code, repl_submitted_code_preview, repl_submitted_code_hash, repl_submitted_code_chars, repl_expanded_code, repl_expanded_code_hash, repl_skill_expansion_meta, repl_did_expand. NOTE: reasoning_output@d{N} uses a separate depth-scoping mechanism (f-string in agent.py output_key) rather than the depth_key() function.

items.name: depth_key() function
items.description: Verify depth_key(key, 0) returns key unchanged, depth_key(key, N>0) returns key@dN.

items.name: parse_depth_key() function
items.description: Verify parse_depth_key() correctly parses key@dN and key@dNfM formats.

items.name: should_capture_state_key() function
items.description: Verify curated key capture logic: explicit keys (6) + prefix patterns (10) correctly filter.

items.name: Child event re-emission tagging
items.description: Verify child events tagged with custom_metadata.rlm_child_event=True and child_depth/child_fanout_idx.

items.name: REPL_STATE_SNAPSHOT
items.description: Read-only state dict exposed to REPL code for introspection. Contains EXPOSED_STATE_KEYS snapshot plus _rlm_depth, _rlm_fanout_idx, _rlm_agent_name. Written to repl.globals (NOT session state).
items.validation_notes: Lives in repl.globals, not session.state. Still a named constant in state.py.

items.name: reasoning_output (output_key)
items.description: Final answer from reasoning agent, written via ADK output_key mechanism. Depth-scoped via f-string (reasoning_output@d{N}), not depth_key(). Read by dispatch.py as fallback completion path. Captured by should_capture_state_key() via 'reasoning_' prefix.
items.reader: dispatch.py (fallback completion), sqlite_tracing.py (via reasoning_ prefix capture)
items.validation_notes: Depth-scoped via direct f-string in agent.py, NOT via depth_key() function.

items.name: final_answer prefix (CURATED_STATE_PREFIXES)
items.description: CURATED_STATE_PREFIXES includes 'final_answer' prefix, meaning should_capture_state_key() would capture any key starting with 'final_answer'. However, no current writer produces keys with this prefix (distinct from 'final_response_text'). Capture prefix with no writer.
items.validation_notes: Orphan capture prefix — no writer exists. Either intended for future use or dead.

items.name: Unicode icon references
items.description: All dashboard icons are unicode: ≡ (U+2261, flow), ◨ (U+25E8, tree), ↓ (U+2193, down), → (U+2192, right), ← (U+2190, left/back). No Material Icons or Quasar icons used.

items.name: Live context banner component
items.description: Pinned banner showing invocation_id, depth, fanout, iteration, and scope-grouped context chips. Includes fallback: 'No active pane context available.'

items.name: Child transcript page title
items.description: 'Child Transcript: {agent_name}' — section title on child pane page.

items.name: Code cell llm_query line — Child Agent chip
items.description: On each llm_query()/llm_query_batched() line, a 'Child Agent (d#:f#)' chip appears right-aligned. Shows child_depth and child_fanout_idx from LlmQueryLineInfo. Bordered with var(--accent-root) cyan, 8px radius. Currently a STATIC label — NOT clickable to open child window. SHOULD be clickable to open /live/session/{sid}/pane/{pid} in new browser tab.
items.handler: MISSING — needs on_click → window.open(child_pane_url) like FlowChildCard's 'Open window' button
items.validation_notes: GAP: Child Agent chip on code line is NOT clickable. The 'Open window' action only exists on the separate FlowChildCard below. The chip should link directly to the child transcript page.

items.name: Code cell llm_query line — rightward arrow (→)
items.description: Unicode → (U+2192) arrow rendered between code content and Child Agent chip on llm_query lines. Color: var(--accent-root) cyan. Visually communicates code paused at this line for child dispatch.

items.name: Code cell llm_query line — clickable line div
items.description: Entire llm_query line div is clickable (cursor: pointer). Background: rgba(87,199,255,0.08). Left border: 3px solid var(--accent-root). click.stop fires on_click_llm_query_line(info) with LlmQueryLineInfo containing child_pane_id, child_depth, child_fanout_idx.

items.name: Child window recursive flow — child has own execute_code + code cell
items.description: When a child window is opened via /live/session/{sid}/pane/{pid}, the child transcript renders the SAME recursive flow grammar as the parent: Agent Card → execute_code arrow → Code Cell → llm_query dispatch → deeper Child Agent chips → return arrows → Output Cell. This means depth+1 children can themselves have llm_query lines with depth+2 Child Agent chips, recursively.
items.validation_notes: Verify: child page renders agent card, execute_code arrow, code cell, and if the child calls llm_query(), those lines show depth+2 Child Agent chips with their own open-window capability.

items.name: FlowChildCard 'Open window' button
items.description: Flat dense button on inline FlowChildCard. Calls on_open_child_window(child) which invokes ui.run_javascript('window.open(...)') to open /live/session/{sid}/pane/{child.pane_id} in new browser tab. Only rendered when on_open_child_window callback is provided AND child.pane_id is set.

items.name: FlowChildCard 'Expand' button
items.description: Flat dense button on inline FlowChildCard. Calls on_expand_child(child) to focus the child pane in the current flow view without opening a new window.

items.name: Child window header lineage badge
items.description: Header in child transcript page showing lineage: 'Parent: d0:froot → Child: d1:f0' (or equivalent). Includes ← Back to session link. Must clearly distinguish child page from root-layer transcript.
items.validation_notes: Verify: lineage badge shows correct parent→child depth/fanout notation. Back link navigates to /live.

items.name: LlmQueryLineInfo.child_pane_id
items.description: Field on LlmQueryLineInfo linking code line to child pane. Required for the Child Agent chip to open the correct child window. Value comes from flow_builder.py matching llm_query lines to child invocations.
items.validation_notes: Verify: child_pane_id is populated correctly by flow_builder.py for each llm_query line.
